task_completion.description: Open the project file 'trail_drape_start.top' in TopoCal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The TopoCal application is open, and the title bar, file explorer, or workspace indicates that 'trail_drape_start.top' has been successfully loaded.

task_completion.description: Generate a triangulated Digital Terrain Model (MDT/TIN) from the survey points.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A triangulated mesh or surface (TIN/MDT) is visible in the TopoCal viewport, connecting the loaded survey points.

task_completion.description: Project the 2D trail polyline onto the active terrain surface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the specific TopoCal menu or tool to project/drape the polyline onto the MDT (expect Spanish UI terms like 'Proyectar').

task_completion.description: Assign real Z-elevations to the polyline.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The polyline is visually shown conforming to the 3D terrain surface in a 3D view, or a properties panel displays non-zero Z-elevation values for the polyline vertices.

task_completion.description: Export the resulting 3D polyline drawing to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\trail_3D.dxf'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or 'Export' dialog is visible showing the destination path 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\trail_3D.dxf' with the DXF file format selected, or a success message confirming the export.

integrity.description: Agent used the TopoCal GUI to process the terrain and polyline.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through TopoCal menus and dialogs rather than executing terminal commands or scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Agent exported the DXF using TopoCal's native functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The DXF file is created via TopoCal's 'Save' or 'Export' dialogs, not by echoing text into a file or using a text editor.

integrity.description: Agent performed the actual triangulation and projection steps.
integrity.visual_evidence: The intermediate steps of generating the MDT and projecting the polyline are visible in the viewport, proving the agent didn't just copy or rename a pre-existing DXF file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: TopoCal's user interface is inherently in Spanish. Expect Spanish menu items and dialogs (e.g., 'Archivo' for File, 'MDT' for Digital Terrain Model, 'Proyectar' for Project).
